dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 5937 IH: Moving American Money Distant from Anti-National Interests Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Carter of Georgia introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform, and in addition to the Committee on Appropriations, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned

form.official-title: To limit the provision of Federal funding to New York City during any period Zohran Mamdani is mayor, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Moving American Money Distant from Anti-National Interests Act or the MAMDANI Act.

section.header: Limitation on Federal funds for New York City if Zohran Mamdani is mayor
section.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, during any period in which Zohran Mamdani is mayor of New York, New York—

paragraph.text: any unobligated Federal funds available for New York, New York, for any purpose are hereby rescinded; and
no Federal funds may be obligated or expended for any purpose to New York, New York.
